free_text: A study of levels in Nd-149 using the (d,p) and (d,t)
reactions.
.Distorted-wave born approximation calculations
Spectroscopic factors were deduced.
The reaction products were analysed in a single
gap Elbek-type magnetic spectrograph and detected in
Ilford K2 nuclear emulsion with a thickness of 25
microns.
The total integrated deuteron beam current
was approximately from 400 muC to 800 muC.
The relative values of the
cross sections for a state measured at different angles
from the same target nucleus should have a better
accuracy and are of statistical nature only about +-10%
SB
SD: Units in col. E-LVL in Subent 3 were
changed (MEV -> KEV). COMMENT renamed on MONITOR and
moved to Subents 2,3.
The absolute cross sections were determined by
normalisation to the cross section for elastic deuteron
scattering.
Evaporated neodymium target
isotopically enriched in Nd-150 up to about 95% were
used. The targets were approximately 110 mug/cm**2
thick, evaporated onto 10-20 mug/cm**2 thick carbon
backing.
*EN-RSL-FW*.The overall energy resolution was 12-15 keV
(FWHM) in detection of the outgoing tritons.
The absolute cross section values (including
statistical error) are not known to better than +-20%
due to uncertainties in normalisation procedure.
*By authors*.
-For ground state for 125 degree in paper the value
22 is wrong, must be 2(1.5)
The data were received from M.Jaskola
for Nd-150(d,t)Nd-149 reactions 04.02.2009
.By M.Jaskola
SD: MONITOR was added.
The absolute cross sections were determined by
normalisation to the cross section for elastic deuteron
scattering.
The absolute cross section values (including
statistical error) are not known to better than +-20%
due to uncertainties in normalisation procedure.
*By authors*.For 148Nd(d,p) reaction only the 60, 90
and 125 degree were measured.
*EN-RSL-FW*.The overall energy resolution was about
2-3 keV (FWHM) in detection of the outgoing protons.
